telegram._comment_channels: Optional group/channel routing — self-serve, no editing needed here. Add the bot as admin to a Telegram group or channel, then POST one of these messages in it: '/link reals' (Gold/Nasdaq/GBPJPY only), '/link deriv' (Deriv synthetics SOL-fib only), '/link ote' (A-grade OTE setups only), '/link alerts' (mirror of everything). The bot fills in realsChannelId / derivChannelId / oteChannelId / alertsChannelId below automatically. All still land in your main chatId too unless you leave it blank.

_comment_whatsapp: Meta WhatsApp Cloud API (official, free). token + phoneNumberId come from the Meta developer dashboard (developers.facebook.com > your app > WhatsApp > API Setup). toNumber is YOUR own WhatsApp number in international format, digits only, no + and no leading 0 (e.g. Nigeria 080... -> "234801..."). Run `node monitor.mjs --setup-whatsapp` after filling these in. IMPORTANT: free-form messages only work within 24h of you messaging the bot's WhatsApp number — message it occasionally (e.g. once a day) to keep alerts flowing; see README.
_comment_timing: alertLeadMinutes = how many minutes BEFORE the 4H close to send the provisional 'forming' heads-up so you can get to the chart (set 0 to only alert at close). confirmAtClose = also send a confirmed/fizzled note once the candle actually closes.
_comment_ltf: ltfEnabled adds a 15m entry plan to each alert: the former + sweeping 4H candle times mapped to your clock, the former-candle liquidity level to hunt, and the exact time of any 15m Sweep Of Liquidity (SOL) found. ltfBufferHours extends the 15m scan window before/after the sweeping candle.
_comment_firstsweep: Stage-2 mid-candle heads-up: fires the instant the forming H4 candle FIRST takes the prior high or low ('manipulation candidate'), with a structured snapshot (wicks, body, close, distance, % elapsed) and the honest base rate that the opposite side completes before close. Noisy by nature — most first sweeps never complete — so it is suppressed once the candle is past firstSweepMaxElapsedPct (where completion odds crater), and only fires for a fresh breach.
_comment_ote: OTE/structure setup alert (swept swing -> strong displacement -> retrace into 0.62-0.79 zone, with FVG confluence). oteTimeframeMin picks the chart it scans: 15 = the 15m entry chart you actually execute on (default); 240 = the 4H structural version validated in ote-study.mjs. oteDispMult = required displacement leg as a multiple of the median candle range (2.5 = strong). oteCandles = how many 15m bars to pull for structure (200 ~= 2 days). NOTE: the ~55-60% backtest was measured on H4 only; on 15m the alert is labelled an unvalidated entry trigger.
_comment_solfib: SYNTHETICS run ONLY the SOL-fib engine (the 4H manipulation flow is OFF for V-pairs). Structure: a SOL (prior swing high/low swept, close back — spike may span several candles, fib 1.0 anchors at the cluster extreme) -> impulse leg (fib 0.0) -> retrace. The reacting level is AGE-ADAPTIVE per the user's charts: fresh SOLs hold 0.618; aged SOLs (>= solFibLongAgeHours since the SOL, default 240h/10d — the V25 June-12 case ran ~a month to 0.886) go deep. Alerts (max 3 per setup): armed at 0.5 retrace (fib map + FVGs tagged with their fib level), 0.618 tap+rejection, 0.886 deep tap+rejection. Invalidation = beyond the SOL extreme (1.0), NOT a tight 0.786 stop. Scans solFibTimeframes minutes (15/30/60). EXPERIMENTAL & UNVALIDATED — encodes the user's charted playbook, labelled honestly.
_comment_solfib_manip: MANIPULATION CONFLUENCE (the stacked-confidence alert): after a setup's 0.618/0.886 was touched, a CLOSED 1H candle that sweeps BOTH sides of its prior bar and closes in the setup's direction (the 4H double-sweep pattern, compressed to 1H) fires a 'CONFLUENCE' alert — location (fib) + confirmation (manipulation). Once per setup; skipped if the target already hit. solFibManipWindowHours = how long after the tap the manipulation still counts as part of the move.
_comment_alignment: bucketOffsetHours shifts 4H candle boundaries for Deriv-sourced instruments. Verified against TradingView/OANDA directly: their forex/metals 4H candles open at 01/05/09/13/17/21 NY, which is the UTC+1h grid — so 1 is the confirmed-correct default, not 0. (TradingView-sourced instruments ignore this; their bars are already correctly aligned.)
_comment_display: How times are shown in alerts. displayTzOffset is whole hours from UTC (New York = -4), displayTzLabel is just the text tag.
